team_context.cap_situation: moderate — ~$48M in total cap space (~$37M effective after Murray dead cap hit), significant dead money from Kyler Murray release ($47-55M depending on pre/post June 1 designation)
team_context.trade_posture: Sellers/retoolers. Coming off a 3-14 season with a new HC (Mike LaFleur), Arizona holds the #3 overall pick and is seeking draft capital and young talent. Veterans on expiring or movable deals could be traded to accelerate the rebuild, but the team also wants to retain its young core (McBride, MHJ, Paris Johnson, Wilson, Will Johnson). Josh Sweat has reportedly asked for a trade following Gannon's firing.

players.name: Jacoby Brissett
players.performance_evidence: In 2025, completed 64.9% of passes for 3,366 yards, 23 TD, 8 INT in 14 games (12 starts). Competent bridge QB who took over after Murray's injury/benching. Not a long-term answer but performed adequately in a bad situation.

trade_value.reasoning: Aging stopgap QB on an expiring deal with modest production. Minimal trade value — a late-round pick at best if a team needs a veteran backup. Cardinals have signaled he's the starter for now but haven't committed.

players.availability_reasoning: Arizona signed Gardner Minshew and is likely drafting a QB at #3 overall. Brissett is a placeholder. If the Cardinals draft a QB, Brissett becomes expendable and could be moved for a conditional late pick.
players.character_notes: Well-regarded veteran leader. Multiple teammates and coaches praised his leadership during the 2025 collapse.
players.trade_buzz: SI reported Brissett could be the Cardinals' biggest trade candidate depending on what happens at QB in free agency/draft.

players.name: Gardner Minshew
players.performance_evidence: Threw just 13 passes in 2025 as Patrick Mahomes' backup in KC. Suffered a late-season knee injury but has a clean bill of health. Career journeyman backup on his 6th team in 7 years.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB on a one-year deal with no guaranteed path to starting. Essentially no trade value — signed days ago as insurance. Only worth considering if a contender suffers a QB injury.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as depth/competition. Could become available if Cardinals draft a QB and don't need a high-priced backup, but more likely stays as the QB2.
players.injury_notes: Suffered knee injury in December 2025 with Chiefs — avoided torn ACL. Clean bill of health entering 2026.

players.name: James Conner
players.performance_evidence: Played only 3 games in 2025 before a season-ending ankle injury in Week 3. Had back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons in 2023-2024 but turns 31 in May. Just restructured to a 1-year, $3M deal — was a cut candidate before the revision.

trade_value.reasoning: 31-year-old RB coming off a season-ending injury on a cheap restructured deal. Minimal trade value. A team needing a veteran RB presence might offer a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Cardinals signed Tyler Allgeier and still have Trey Benson. Conner was restructured to stay as a veteran presence for the locker room, but he's on the back end of his career. If a team wanted him, Arizona would move him for anything of value.
players.injury_notes: Season-ending ankle injury in Week 3 of 2025. History of injuries throughout career. Turned 31.
players.character_notes: Beloved team captain and locker room leader. Cancer survivor. His leadership value exceeds his on-field value at this stage.

players.name: Tyler Allgeier
players.performance_evidence: Had 514 yards and 8 TD on 143 carries as Bijan Robinson's backup in Atlanta in 2025. Rushed for 1,035 yards as a rookie in 2022. Physical downhill runner who has never fumbled or missed a game in his NFL career.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid young RB on a reasonable 2-year deal with $8M guaranteed. Dead cap ($8M) makes a trade impractical in Year 1. Worth a late 4th/early 5th on talent and contract, but guaranteed money essentially locks him in for 2026.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed 3 days ago — Cardinals aren't trading him. Dead cap of $8M exceeds his cap hit, making a trade financially nonsensical. Listed as available at a premium because technically he has a price, but it's unrealistically high for an RB.
players.injury_notes: Never missed a game in 4 NFL seasons. Iron man durability.

players.name: Marvin Harrison Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Disappointing through 2 NFL seasons: 62-885-8 as a rookie in 2024, then just 41-608-4 in 12 games in 2025 (missed 5 games to appendicitis, heel injury, and foot injury). PFF overall grade of 68.4 in 2025 (47th/81 WRs). Has not lived up to #4 overall pick billing yet, but talent ceiling remains very high.

trade_value.reasoning: Despite underperformance, MHJ was the #4 pick just 2 years ago and is only 24 with 2 cheap years left plus a 5th-year option. Dead cap of $20.9M makes a trade extremely unlikely. His draft pedigree and age mean his value is still equivalent to a late 1st-round pick to teams who believe in the talent.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $20.9M makes trading Harrison financially devastating. Cardinals still view him as a franchise cornerstone entering Year 3. New coaching staff under LaFleur should help unlock his potential. Arizona would never move him barring an unprecedented offer.
players.injury_notes: Missed 5 games in 2025 due to appendicitis (Weeks 11-12), heel injury (Weeks 14-15), and foot injury (Week 17+). Two seasons of nagging injuries are a mild concern but nothing structural.

players.name: Michael Wilson
players.performance_evidence: Broke out in 2025 with 78 receptions for 1,006 yards and 7 TD — career highs across the board. Especially dominant in the 2nd half of the season (775 yards and 6 TD in final 8 games). Prior to 2025, had just 500+ yards combined in his first two seasons. Extension candidate — Cardinals have publicly stated he's a priority.

trade_value.reasoning: Breakout 1,000-yard WR entering the final year of a bargain rookie deal at just $4M cap hit. Only 26. Extension-eligible and the Cardinals want to keep him long-term. On raw value, he's worth a 2nd-round pick, but Arizona has zero incentive to trade their emerging WR1.

players.availability_reasoning: Cardinals publicly stated Wilson is a top priority for an extension. He's their best offensive weapon alongside McBride and is on a dirt-cheap final-year deal. Zero dead cap means he COULD be traded easily, but Arizona won't do it — he's foundational to their rebuild.

players.name: Kendrick Bourne
players.performance_evidence: Had 37 receptions for 551 yards in 16 games with the 49ers in 2025. Veteran slot receiver with 329 career receptions and 4,265 career yards. Best season was 2021 (55 catches, 800 yards, 5 TD). LaFleur connection from their shared time in SF.

trade_value.reasoning: 31-year-old WR3 just signed to a modest deal. Dead cap exceeds cap hit in Year 1, making a trade impractical. Minimal standalone trade value — a 7th-round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed but is a veteran addition, not a core piece. If a better option emerged or the team needed cap flexibility, Bourne could be moved, though the guaranteed money makes it unlikely in 2026.
players.character_notes: Well-liked veteran presence. Strong relationship with new HC Mike LaFleur from their 49ers days.

players.name: Trey McBride
players.performance_evidence: First in the NFL among TEs in targets per route run (30.0%), team target share (29.6%), and receiving first downs (63) in 2024. Had 111 receptions for 1,146 yards — 2nd-most receptions in Cardinals franchise history. First Cardinals TE to make a Pro Bowl since 1970. Highest-paid TE in NFL history at $19M AAV.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite young TE on a long-term deal with 4 years of control. Only 25. Top 2-3 at his position. Dead cap of $23.8M makes a trade functionally impossible. Worth multiple 1st-round picks in a hypothetical scenario.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise cornerstone. Just signed a record-setting 4-year, $76M extension. Dead cap of $23.8M makes trading him financially impossible. He IS the Cardinals' offense. Zero chance of a trade.

players.name: Tip Reiman
players.performance_evidence: Appeared in 17 games (9 starts) as a rookie in 2024 with just 6 catches for 37 yards, but was a key blocker — played 40%+ of offensive snaps. Lost to injury in 2025 season. Valued as an inline blocking TE in 12-personnel packages.

trade_value.reasoning: Young blocking TE on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years left. Very limited receiving production limits his value. Worth a late-round pick to a team that values blocking TEs.

players.availability_reasoning: With McBride locked up long-term, Reiman is a useful complementary piece but not irreplaceable. If a team offered a Day 3 pick, Arizona would consider it, though they value the 12-personnel package.
players.injury_notes: Lost for the season during 2025. Nature of injury not widely specified.

players.name: Paris Johnson Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Has started all 51 games in his first 3 NFL seasons at LT. 5th-year option decision coming in May 2026 (projected $19.1M for 2027). GM Ossenfort praised him at the Combine as a foundational piece. Solid but still improving — not yet Pro Bowl caliber.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting LT on a cheap Year 4 of his rookie deal with a 5th-year option. Former #6 overall pick, only 24. Position premium (OT) and age make him extremely valuable. Dead cap equals his cap hit, so a trade saves nothing — and the Cardinals have him as a building block.

players.availability_reasoning: Cardinals publicly stated Johnson is a long-term priority. Extension-eligible now, with 5th-year option decision imminent. Young franchise LT on a cheap deal — there is no realistic price that makes trading him worthwhile during a rebuild.
players.injury_notes: Remarkably durable — hasn't missed a game in 3 seasons.

players.name: Isaac Seumalo
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl selection in 2024. Allowed the lowest pressure rate among all guards in 2025 (3.7% per Next Gen Stats). 73.3 PFF grade in 2025. 10-year vet with 125 games and 103 starts. Super Bowl champion with Eagles (2017).

trade_value.reasoning: Quality veteran guard on a new 3-year deal, but just signed days ago with $16M in guaranteed money. Dead cap makes a trade impossible in 2026. On talent/contract alone worth a 3rd-round pick, but he's staying put.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to be the anchor of the OL rebuild. Dead cap of $16M makes a trade impossible in Year 1. He's here to start. Would only become available if the Cardinals made a radical roster pivot, which isn't happening.
players.injury_notes: Missed 7 games across last 4 years. Dealt with two different injuries in 2025 that kept him out of 3 games.
players.character_notes: Veteran leader. Super Bowl champion. Cited desire to 'win with a team that wants me here' when explaining Cardinals signing.

players.name: Hjalte Froholdt
players.performance_evidence: Played all 17 games in 2024 (1,123 offensive snaps). PFF grades improved significantly down the stretch. Extended in August 2024 on a 2-year, $12M deal with $8M guaranteed. Entering final year of that extension.

trade_value.reasoning: Average starting center on the final year of a reasonable deal. $7M cap hit is fair for a starter. Only $2M dead cap makes him movable, but there's limited demand for mid-tier centers. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his deal, manageable cap hit, low dead cap. If the Cardinals draft or sign a center upgrade, Froholdt becomes tradeable. Currently the starter but not a core building block for the long term.

players.name: Isaiah Adams
players.performance_evidence: Started as a rookie in 2024 at RG after being drafted in the 3rd round (71st overall) out of Illinois. Still developing — showed flashes but was part of a struggling OL unit. May face competition from Seumalo or Matt Pryor for the starting RG role in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Young developing guard on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years left. Upside as a starter but hasn't proved it yet. Worth a 5th-round pick to a team needing IOL depth with development potential.

players.availability_reasoning: With Seumalo signed, Adams may lose his starting job. He's cheap and young enough to have value as a trade chip if Arizona decides to move on. Could be packaged in a deal for additional draft capital.

players.name: Josh Fryar
players.performance_evidence: Thrust into starting duty during a chaotic 2025 OL season. RT was identified as a major position of need going into the offseason. Fryar is a stopgap — the Cardinals may draft or sign a RT upgrade.

trade_value.reasoning: Young swing tackle on a minimum deal with 2 years of control. Below-average starter but cheap. Worth a late-round pick as OL depth.

players.availability_reasoning: RT is a known position of need. If Arizona adds a starter via the draft or free agency, Fryar becomes a depth piece who could be moved for a late pick.

players.name: Josh Sweat
players.performance_evidence: Had a career-best 12 sacks and 4 forced fumbles in 2025 — one of the few bright spots on a bad Cardinals team. Led the Eagles in sacks (8) in 2024 and had 2.5 sacks in the Super Bowl. Super Bowl champion. Consistent double-digit sack threat.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite-level production (12 sacks) at a premium position (EDGE) with 3 years of control at $19.1M AAV — a fair price. However, $31.8M dead cap in 2026 makes a trade THIS YEAR essentially impossible. If traded post-June 1, dead cap could be spread. Worth a 1st-round pick to a contender, but logistics are extremely difficult.

players.availability_reasoning: Sweat reportedly asked for a trade following Jonathan Gannon's firing. However, the $31.8M dead cap makes a pre-June 1 trade financially devastating. DC Nick Rallis was retained, which may ease Sweat's concerns. If the Cardinals find a way to make the cap math work (post-June 1), he could be dealt for significant draft capital.
players.character_notes: Reportedly unhappy about Gannon's firing — he signed with Arizona specifically to reunite with Gannon. May have settled after DC Rallis was retained.
players.trade_buzz: PHNX Cardinals podcast reported Sweat asked for a trade when Gannon was fired. Bears and other teams have been discussed as potential suitors. The dead cap situation is the primary obstacle.

players.name: Roy Lopez
players.performance_evidence: Had 30 tackles, 2 sacks, and 4 TFL in 17 games with the Lions in 2025. Outperformed higher-paid DTs in Detroit in sacks, QB hits, and TFL. PFF credited him with 13 pressures and a 63.6 pass rush grade. Solid run-stuffing DT with emerging pass rush ability.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starting DT on a reasonable 2-year deal, but just signed with $7M guaranteed that exceeds his 2026 cap hit in dead cap. Essentially untradeable in Year 1. Worth a 4th/5th-round pick on talent alone.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed with $7M guaranteed. Dead cap exceeds cap hit, making a 2026 trade impractical. Cardinals brought him back specifically for scheme familiarity with DC Rallis.
players.character_notes: Hometown kid — grew up in Gilbert, AZ. Deep ties to the community. Cardinals family connection with DC Rallis.

players.name: Darius Robinson
players.performance_evidence: Missed most of his rookie season (2024) with a calf injury — played only 6 games, recording 10 tackles and 1 sack. Was the final first-round pick from the 2024 class to debut. Still largely unproven at the NFL level but has immense physical tools (4.95 forty at 285 lbs). Cardinals are banking on a breakout in 2025-26.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick still on a cheap rookie deal with a 5th-year option. Mostly untested due to injuries but the physical talent is undeniable. Worth an early-to-mid 2nd based on draft pedigree, age, and contract alone — but the Cardinals aren't selling low on a 1st-rounder entering his prove-it year.

players.availability_reasoning: The Cardinals invested significant draft capital (traded up for pick 27) and Robinson still has a 5th-year option. They need to see what they have in him before making any decision. Would only move him for an overpay — think a 1st-round pick.
players.injury_notes: Calf injury cost him most of 2024 season. Was on IR from August through late October. Health is the key question.
players.character_notes: Excellent character. Master's degree in Education. Started his own foundation.

players.name: Cody Simon
players.performance_evidence: 2025 4th-round pick out of Ohio State. Started as a rookie and established himself as one of the starting ILBs. Cardinals are expected to enter 2026 with Simon and Mack Wilson as their starting duo.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting LB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Limited pedigree (4th round) and LB is a lower-value position. Worth a 5th/6th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, and starting — the Cardinals have no reason to move him. He's exactly the type of player you keep during a rebuild. Would need an overpay to pry him loose.

players.name: Mack Wilson Sr.
players.performance_evidence: Team captain and starting ILB. Solid run defender and reliable tackler but limited in coverage. Cardinals considered upgrading at LB but are running it back with Wilson and Simon as starters in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Average starting LB on an expiring deal. Low positional value. Worth a late-round pick to a team needing LB depth, but the market for average LBs is thin.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract, below-average starter at a replaceable position. Cardinals would trade him for fair value — a 5th or 6th-round pick. If they draft LB talent, Wilson becomes immediately available.
players.character_notes: Team captain. Respected veteran voice in the locker room.

players.name: Zaven Collins
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick who has never lived up to his draft billing. Has bounced between LB and EDGE roles without excelling at either. Has some pass-rush ability but is a below-average coverage LB. Entering a contract year.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round bust on an expiring deal with $0 dead cap. Very easy to trade from a cap standpoint. Worth a 6th-round pick to a team that thinks a change of scenery could unlock his athleticism.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring deal, zero dead cap, has never met expectations. Cardinals have no long-term investment in Collins. If any team wants him for a late-round pick, Arizona would happily oblige.

players.name: Will Johnson
players.performance_evidence: 2025 2nd-round pick out of Michigan who was thrust into a starting role as a rookie when Sean Murphy-Bunting and Starling Thomas V were lost before the season. Had strong performances despite the trial-by-fire. The Cardinals view him as a foundational piece in the secondary alongside Garrett Williams and Denzel Burke.

trade_value.reasoning: Young CB1 on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control at a premium position. Only 22 years old. Draft pedigree (2nd round) and age premium make him very valuable. Worth an early 2nd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: The Cardinals invested a high 2nd-round pick in Johnson just last year and he's already starting. He's part of the young core the team is building around. No chance Arizona moves him unless overwhelmed with an offer.
players.injury_notes: Fell in 2025 draft due to knee concerns from Michigan. Missed 5 games as a rookie.

players.name: Denzel Burke
players.performance_evidence: 2025 5th-round pick out of Ohio State. Was thrown into the fire as a rookie alongside Will Johnson after Murphy-Bunting and Thomas were lost. Had some strong performances and earned a starting role. Had 48 tackles, 2 INTs, and 2 PDs in his final season at Ohio State.

trade_value.reasoning: Young CB on a minimum-level rookie deal with 3 years of control. Late-round pedigree limits his perceived value, but he's already starting. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, and starting at a premium position. Cardinals need CB depth and aren't looking to subtract from the secondary. Would need an overpay to move him.
players.character_notes: Local kid — attended Brophy Prep and Saguaro High School in the Phoenix area before heading to Ohio State.

players.name: Garrett Williams
players.performance_evidence: Was one of the league's best slot CBs in 2024 before injuries derailed his 2025 season. Missed 5 games with a knee injury, returned, then tore his Achilles late in the year. In 10 games in 2025, had 46 tackles, 1 INT, 5 PDs, and a forced fumble. Extension-eligible entering final year of his rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: When healthy, Williams is a top-tier slot CB on a very cheap deal. But the Achilles tear is a serious concern entering 2026. Expiring deal adds risk for any acquirer. Worth a 3rd-round pick if healthy, but the Achilles drops him to a 4th-round equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his deal, no dead cap, and coming off a torn Achilles. The Cardinals may want to see if he can return to form before extending him. If they don't believe he'll recover fully, they could trade him for a Day 3 pick rather than lose him for nothing in free agency.
players.injury_notes: Tore his Achilles late in the 2025 season. Missed 5 games earlier in the year with a knee injury. Availability for the start of 2026 is uncertain.

players.name: Dadrion Taylor-Demerson
players.performance_evidence: Appeared in all 17 games as a rookie in 2024. With Jalen Thompson departing for Dallas in free agency, Taylor-Demerson is expected to slide into the starting SS role alongside Budda Baker in 2026. Young and developing.

trade_value.reasoning: Young safety on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years of control. Projected to start in 2026. Limited track record but good upside. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: The Cardinals just lost Jalen Thompson in free agency and are counting on Taylor-Demerson to start. They have no incentive to trade him. Would need an overpay.

players.name: Budda Baker
players.performance_evidence: 6x Pro Bowler, 2x First-Team All-Pro. Entering his 10th season, all with Arizona. Approaching franchise legend status — nearing Larry Fitzgerald tier of importance to the organization. Still producing at a high level as a box safety/run defender, though coverage skills have declined with age.

trade_value.reasoning: Still a Pro Bowl-caliber safety but turns 30 in January 2027 with a $16.1M cap hit and $12.8M dead cap. An acquiring team would take on ~$16M/year for 2 years. Worth a 2nd-round pick to a contender, but the contract and age limit the market. Dead cap of $12.8M means the Cardinals eat significant money even if they trade him.

players.availability_reasoning: Baker is a franchise icon but the Cardinals went 3-14 and are rebuilding. He previously requested a trade in 2023. At 30, his timeline doesn't match Arizona's competitive window. The Bears and other teams have been linked. If a contender offers a 2nd-round pick, the Cardinals would seriously consider it — but they won't give him away.
players.character_notes: Franchise icon. Team captain. Beloved in the community. Previously requested a trade in April 2023 before getting an extension in December 2024.
players.trade_buzz: Bears were linked as a potential trade partner for Baker in February 2026. Multiple outlets have noted his timeline doesn't match Arizona's rebuild, but the Cardinals retain him as a leader. His contract structure makes a trade complicated but not impossible.

players.name: Elijah Wilkinson
players.performance_evidence: Journeyman offensive lineman who has bounced between Denver, Chicago, Las Vegas, and Arizona. Has started games at both guard and tackle throughout his career. Serviceable but below-average pass protector who has allowed pressures at a higher-than-ideal rate. PFF grades have generally hovered in the low-to-mid 50s range in recent seasons. Fills a role as a swing tackle/depth piece who can start in a pinch but is not a long-term answer at right tackle. Won the starting RT job in Arizona largely due to lack of better options rather than dominant play.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting OT on a cheap one-year deal with no guarantees. Minimal trade value — a team desperate for OL depth might offer a 7th-round pick, but most teams can find equivalent players on the street. UDFA pedigree and age (29) further limit interest.

players.availability_reasoning: Low-cost depth piece on an expiring deal. Arizona would part with him for any draft compensation as he is a placeholder rather than a building block. If the Cardinals draft or acquire a better RT option, Wilkinson becomes expendable immediately.

players.name: Andrew Wingard
players.performance_evidence: Spent his first five NFL seasons with Jacksonville, primarily as a special teams contributor and rotational safety before earning more playing time. Signed with Arizona as a veteran depth/starter option. A capable run-support safety and reliable tackler who has posted solid tackle numbers when given playing time (80+ tackles in his heavier-snap seasons). However, he is limited in coverage, particularly in man situations, with PFF coverage grades generally in the 50s-low 60s range. He's a below-average starter who contributes effort and physicality but does not create turnovers at a meaningful rate.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting safety on a minimum-level expiring deal. Safety is already a lower-value position, and Wingard's limitations in coverage further suppress value. Would be hard-pressed to return anything more than a late 7th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Arizona is in the early stages of a competitive build and would prefer to develop younger options at safety. Wingard is a stopgap on a one-year deal with zero dead cap. If any team offered even a conditional late-round pick, Arizona would likely accept. He is the type of veteran who could be replaced internally or through the draft without any drop-off.
